skills.name: CS2 counter strike
skills.evidence: Reported 5,400 hours of playtime since 2012 and a Premier rating of 23k. Demonstrated foundational strategic knowledge, including using standard CT jump-spotting on Mirage B site, basic spawn-corner jump-throw utility setups for Ancient, and anti-flash/smoke-spraying mechanics to stall rushes on Inferno.

assessment.summary: With 5.4k hours and a 23k Premier rating, the user sits in the elite bracket of matchmaking. While they possess strong foundational habits (anti-flash, jump-spotting, standard jump-throws), their responses were highly generalized, showing passive positioning habits and a lack of depth on newer active maps like Anubis.
strengths: Excellent raw game sense and positioning habits (playing anti-flash and utilizing smoke spamming to stop rushes)
High level of experience and playtime (5,400+ hours since 2012)
Solid basic utility knowledge, utilizing standard spawn-corner jump-throw lineups on Ancient
gaps: Passive anchoring habits on eco rounds (relying on hiding rather than proactive high-risk/high-reward plays)
Map pool gaps, specifically lacking tactical familiarity with Anubis
Gave generalized tactical answers rather than highly coordinated team-play setups

skills.name: QA automation skill
skills.evidence:
AI assessment (medium confidence): Reliable intermediate: independent routine UI/API automation and issue detection on common stacks. Good judgment on API vs UI and security. Gaps in advanced flakiness mitigation (relies on static waits) and performance tools like Gatling. Handles common situations well but limited evidence of complex non-routine diagnosis.

Evidence: Java/Gradle/Playwright (post-Selenide) for realtime dashboard filtering tests discovering bugs (dates, search, operators); custom Jenkins failure tracker; security-aware login tests with request checks; API prioritization over UI due to stability.

skills.name: Jenkins
skills.evidence: Maintains Jenkins freestyle jobs for roughly 15 repositories with production and development variants. Independently created a job for a QA failure-tracking tool, configured multi-service setups involving Elasticsearch, chained jobs, JaCoCo coverage jobs, and post-deployment migration jobs. Correctly distinguished build triggers, build steps, and post-build actions, and described logging checked-o…

assessment.summary: Demonstrates independent ability to maintain and create routine Jenkins freestyle jobs, supporting level 4. Experience is concrete rather than vocabulary-only. Important gaps prevent an intermediate level 5 rating: limited understanding of credentials and secret protection, concurrency and duplicate-run prevention, agent/environment diagnosis, deployment safeguards, and non-routine troubleshooting. Knowledge appears concentrated in application-facing freestyle configuration, with infrastructure and deeper Jenkins administration handled by DevOps.

quests.title: Build and optimize a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Java application
quests.notes: Create a Dockerfile that separates the build environment from the runtime environment to minimize image size and improve security.

quests.title: Integrate Docker container execution into a Jenkins CI/CD pipeline
quests.notes: Configure a Jenkins pipeline to spin up a Docker container, execute QA automation tests inside it, and clean up the container afterward.
